nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: 峡谷峭壁
nodes.summary: 大型猛禽和食腐鸟偏好的高崖栖息环境。

nodes.name: Chocó–Darién moist forests
nodes.summary: Chocó–Darién moist forests是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Scarlet-and-white tanager，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Scarlet-and-white tanager
nodes.summary: Scarlet-and-white tanager（Scarlet-and-white tanager，学名 Chrysothlypis salmoni），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括峡谷峭壁，主要威胁包括森林砍伐。

nodes.name: 哥倫比亞中部山脈
nodes.summary: 哥倫比亞中部山脈是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联25种鸟类，包括黄耳鹦哥、Choco manakin、Barred fruiteater等25种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 厄瓜多尔
nodes.summary: 厄瓜多尔是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联857种鸟类，包括安第斯斑䳍、红头林䳍、灰䳍等857种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 哥伦比亚
nodes.summary: 哥伦比亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联947种鸟类，包括红头林䳍、灰䳍、白喉䳍等947种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is found in Colombia and northern Ecuador

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is found in Colombia and northern Ecuador

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: However, it can be found at elevations of up to in the Chocó of the Cordillera Central in Colombia. It inhabits stunted secondary growth on foothills, especially around dense, mossy woodland around ridge tops, steep canyon slopes, or landslide scars

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: However, it can be found at elevations of up to in the Chocó of the Cordillera Central in Colombia. It inhabits stunted secondary growth on foothills, especially around dense, mossy woodland around ridge tops, steep canyon slopes, or landslide scars

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: However, it can be found at elevations of up to in the Chocó of the Cordillera Central in Colombia. It inhabits stunted secondary growth on foothills, especially around dense, mossy woodland around ridge tops, steep canyon slopes, or landslide scars

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: It is threatened by increasing deforestation throughout its range, especially in foothills, but is unlikely to be in immediate danger due to its affinity for disturbed habitats
